perturbation.original_text: For the performance of the Adviser's obligations under this Agreement, including payment of certain expenses of each of the Funds pursuant to paragraph 3 hereof, each Fund shall pay the Adviser, on the first business day following the end of each month, a fee at an annual rate of 0.65% of a Fund's average daily net assets up to $25 million, 0.35% of a Fund's average daily net assets from $25 million to $100 million, and 0.25% of a Fund's average daily net assets for such assets in excess of $100 million.
perturbation.changed_text: For the performance of the Adviser's obligations under this Agreement, including payment of certain expenses of each of the Funds pursuant to paragraph 3 hereof, each Fund shall pay the Adviser, on the first business day following the end of each month, a fee at an annual rate of 0.65% of a Fund's average daily net assets, regardless of the total amount of assets under management.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the tiered fee structure based on asset size. The original clause specifies decreasing percentages for asset levels exceeding $25 million and $100 million. The modified clause mandates a flat 0.65% fee, irrespective of asset size, which directly contradicts the fee structure outlined in the original text in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation. For the performance of the Adviser's obligations under this Agreement, including payment of certain expenses of each of the Funds pursuant to paragraph 3 hereof, each Fund shall pay the Adviser, on the first business day following the end of each month, a fee at an annual rate of 0.65% of a Fund's average daily net assets, regardless of the total amount of assets under management. The average value of the daily net assets of a Fund shall be determined pursuant to the applicable provisions of the Agreement and Declaration of Trust or a resolution of the Board of Trustees, if required. If, pursuant to such provisions, the determination of net asset value of a Fund is suspended for any particular business day, then for the purposes of this paragraph, the value of the net assets of a Fund as last determined shall be deemed to be the value of the net assets as of the close of the business day, or as of such other time as the value of the Fund's net assets may lawfully be determined, on that day. If the determination of the net asset value of a Fund has been suspended for a period including such month, the Adviser's compensation payable at the end of such month shall be computed on the basis of the value of the net assets of the Fund as last determined (whether during or prior to such month).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the tiered fee structure with a flat fee, directly contradicting the original clause that outlines decreasing percentages for asset levels exceeding specified thresholds. The contradicted text repeats the flat fee. Since the change directly alters the compensation calculation from a tiered system to a flat rate, and is repeated in the contradicted text, a strong in-text contradiction exists because these two texts cannot both be true.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated without the payment of any penalty by either party upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by the Trust without the payment of any penalty upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the Adviser. However, if the Adviser terminates the agreement, a penalty of $50,000 will be applied.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by creating asymmetry in the termination clause. Originally, either party could terminate without penalty. The modification adds a $50,000 penalty specifically for the Adviser's termination, directly contradicting the initial statement that termination can occur 'without the payment of any penalty by either party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Renewal and Termination. The term of this Services Agreement shall begin on the date of execution and shall continue in effect for a period of two years. This Services Agreement shall continue in effect from year to year thereafter, subject to termination as hereinafter provided, if such continuance is approved at least annually by vote of a majority of the Trustees who are not interested persons of the Trust or the Adviser. This Agreement may be terminated by the Trust without the payment of any penalty upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the Adviser. However, if the Adviser terminates the agreement, a penalty of $50,000 will be applied. Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Trust shall pay the Adviser such compensation as may be payable for the period prior to the effective date of such termination. This Agreement shall automatically terminate in the event the Management Agreement is assigned or otherwise terminated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by creating asymmetry in the termination clause. Originally, either party could terminate without penalty as explicitly stated in the original text: "This Agreement may be terminated without the payment of any penalty by either party upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party." The modification adds a $50,000 penalty specifically for the Adviser's termination, directly contradicting the initial statement that termination can occur 'without the payment of any penalty by either party', as well as the repeated statement in the contradicted text that "This Agreement may be terminated by the Trust without the payment of any penalty upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the Adviser.". This creates an inconsistency as described in the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records in accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to, records required by Section 31(a) of the Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the various functions performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party pursuant to contract with the Trust.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records, but is not responsible for maintaining these records if its is unduly burdensome or costly. If those records are deemed unduly burdensome or costly, it is the Trust's responsibility. In accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to, records required by Section 31(a) of the Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the various functions performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party pursuant to contract with the Trust.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a caveat that allows the Adviser to avoid maintaining records if deemed 'unduly burdensome or costly', shifting responsibility to the Trust. This directly contradicts the initial unequivocal statement that 'The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records'. This undermines the enforceability of the record-keeping obligation and introduces ambiguity regarding responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Record Keeping and Other Information. The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records, but is not responsible for maintaining these records if its is unduly burdensome or costly. If those records are deemed unduly burdensome or costly, it is the Trust's responsibility. In accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to, records required by Section 31(a) of the Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the various functions performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party pursuant to contract with the Trust. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by the Adviser for the periods and in the places required by Rule 31a-2 under the Act.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the Adviser is not responsible for maintaining records if it is unduly burdensome or costly, shifting the responsibility to the Trust. This directly contradicts the earlier statement in both the original and contradicted text, which initially mandates that 'The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records.' The added condition undermines the enforceability of the original record-keeping obligation, thus causing a contradiction.
